children.label: A
children.text: reducing the risks associated with the transportation of hazardous material; and

children.label: B
children.text: identifying and evaluating new technologies to facilitate the safe, secure, and efficient transportation of hazardous material.

children.label: A
children.text: utilize information gathered from other modal administrations with similar programs;

children.label: B
children.text: coordinate with other modal administrations, as appropriate; and

children.label: C
children.text: coordinate, as appropriate, with other Federal agencies.

children.heading: .—Cooperation
children.label: b .—Cooperation
children.text: In carrying out subsection (a), the Secretary shall work cooperatively with regulated and other entities, including shippers, carriers, emergency responders, State and local officials, and academic institutions.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: As part of the program established under subsection (a), the Secretary may carry out cooperative research on hazardous materials transport.

children.heading: .—National academies
children.label: 2 .—National academies
children.text: The Secretary may enter into an agreement with the National Academies to support research described in paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: emergency planning and response, including information and programs that can be readily assessed and implemented in local jurisdictions;

children.label: B
children.text: risk analysis and perception and data assessment;

children.label: C
children.text: commodity flow data, including voluntary collaboration between shippers and first responders for secure data exchange of critical information;
